_comment: Example Comment with a multi-round thread. Demonstrates the threaded conversation model from D-12: instruction → ai-proposal → follow-up → ai-proposal. Use as a fixture for testing the comment system (M5).
quotedText: EU industrial-decarbonization tax credits for SMR offtake expire Q4 2027.

thread.author: Jane Smith
thread.text: Make this title action-oriented and quantify the urgency.

block.title: Decide by Q1 2027 to secure €40M in first-mover tax credits

content.text: EU industrial-decarbonization tax credits for SMR offtake expire Q4 2027, leaving a 9-month window to lock in pricing.

patch.reason: Quantified urgency in the title (€40M) and added 9-month window.

thread.author: Jane Smith
thread.text: The €40M figure isn't confirmed. Use 'significant' instead, and keep the timing concrete.

block.title: Decide by Q1 2027 to secure significant first-mover tax credits

content.text: EU industrial-decarbonization tax credits for SMR offtake expire Q4 2027, leaving a 9-month window to lock in pricing.

patch.reason: Replaced '€40M' with 'significant'; kept the Q1 2027 / 9-month framing.

_usage_notes: The thread always starts with an 'instruction' entry from the consultant.
Each AI response is an 'ai-proposal' entry containing a BlockPatch + token usage.
Follow-ups are 'follow-up' entries from the consultant — they queue locally until the next batch submit.
The 'current proposal' is always the LAST 'ai-proposal' in the thread — that's the one Accept/Reject acts on.
Comments can be in three statuses: 'open' (under review), 'applied' (consultant accepted), 'rejected'.
When the LLM responds, the full thread is passed in as context — it sees all prior exchanges.
